policy_refs: [CS-RULE-001, CS-RULE-002, CS-RULE-003, CS-RULE-004, CS-RULE-005, SEC-RULE-002, SEC-RULE-003, CS-RULE-007, CS-RULE-018, CS-RULE-019, CS-RULE-020, CS-RULE-021]
expected_outcome: Plan upgraded from 100 Mbps to 500 Mbps effective immediately. Pro-rated charge for current cycle disclosed. No ticket required. Survey offered.

coverage.notes: account number + last 4 of payment + full name — PASS

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thank you for calling NexaLink Telecommunications Broadband and Support. My name is Priya. Who do I have the pleasure of speaking with today?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: And this call may be recorded for quality assurance and training purposes.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Hi Priya, this is James Albright. I'm thinking about upgrading my internet plan and wanted to talk through the options.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Hello, Mr. Albright. Happy to help with that.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I understand you're calling regarding a plan upgrade, and I can certainly help you with that. Before I can pull up your account, I'll need to verify a few details. Could you start with your account number?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Sure. Seven-two-zero-four, one-eight-six-five, two-two.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thank you. And could you confirm the last four digits of the payment method on file?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Last four are oh-nine-five-seven.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Perfect. And finally — could you confirm the full name on the account?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: James Robert Albright.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: That matches. You're fully verified, Mr. Albright.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Great.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Your data is used solely for service provisioning and support quality, in accordance with our Privacy Policy.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Understood.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: So I can see you're currently on our hundred-megabit Home Essentials plan at fifty-nine dollars a month. What's driving the interest in upgrading?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: We've got two teenagers and both my wife and I work from home now. The hundred is just not cutting it on video calls.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: That makes a lot of sense — four heavy users on a hundred-megabit line is genuinely tight, especially with video. Let me walk you through what's available.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Sure, go ahead.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: The next tier up is our Home Pro plan at three hundred megabits, sixty-nine dollars a month. Above that is Home Pro Plus at five hundred megabits, seventy-nine a month. And our top consumer tier is Gigabit Home at a thousand megabits, ninety-nine a month. All three include the same equipment and the same unlimited data.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Hmm. What would you recommend for two work-from-home adults plus two teenagers gaming and streaming?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Honestly, the five-hundred plan tends to be the sweet spot for households like yours. It handles simultaneous four-K streams and video calls comfortably without paying for headroom you wouldn't use. Gigabit is real, but most households don't saturate it.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Okay, let's go with the five hundred.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Excellent choice. So I'll be moving you from Home Essentials at fifty-nine dollars to Home Pro Plus at seventy-nine dollars, an increase of twenty dollars on your monthly bill.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Right.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Since we're partway through your current billing cycle, this cycle will show a small pro-rated charge for the upgraded plan from today through your bill date. That comes out to about thirteen dollars and thirty cents. Your next full bill will then show the new plan at seventy-nine dollars.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: And the upgraded speed — when does that kick in?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: As soon as I apply the change, the new speed is provisioned on your modem within about fifteen minutes. You may need to power-cycle the router once for it to pick up the higher cap.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Okay. Yeah, let's do it.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Confirming: upgrade to Home Pro Plus, five hundred megabits, seventy-nine dollars monthly, effective immediately, with a thirteen-dollar-thirty cent pro-rated charge on the current cycle. Is that correct?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Yes, that's right.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Applying it now.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: And it's done. The new plan is live on your account.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Wow, that was fast.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: To summarize: I upgraded your Home Essentials plan to Home Pro Plus at five hundred megabits per second. A pro-rated charge of thirteen dollars and thirty cents will appear on this cycle's bill, and from next cycle onward you'll see the seventy-nine dollar monthly rate. The new speed should be active within fifteen minutes once you power-cycle the router. Does that all sound right?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Sounds perfect. Thank you.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: My pleasure. Is there anything else I can help you with today, Mr. Albright?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: No, that's it. Appreciate the help.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Your case reference number is N-X-L dash seven-seven-two-zero-five-five-eight. You'll receive a brief survey after we hang up — if you have a moment, we'd really appreciate the feedback. Thank you for being a NexaLink customer, Mr. Albright. Have a great rest of your day.
